q: Things are going well. Your brain says:
options: 'This won't last'
'I don't deserve this'
'Let me find a flaw before they do'
'What if I'm only successful by accident?'

q: You're close to achieving something big:
options: I slow down or stop completely
I pick a fight or create drama
I raise the bar so I can't possibly reach it
I downplay it so no one expects more
I wait for catastrophe to motivate me
I convince myself it doesn't matter anyway
I redo everything until nothing gets finished
I'm scared the result will prove I'm not good enough

q: Someone gives you a genuine compliment:
options: I deflect immediately
I think they're being polite
I find something they missed
I wonder when they'll realize they're wrong
It's temporary and danger follows
It's for other people, not really for me
It's never enough — there's always more to fix
It happened by luck, not merit
See? I knew it was only a matter of time
This proves what I've always feared about myself
I can't stop replaying what I should've done
I hope no one noticed

q: How do you handle good news?
options: Wait for the other shoe to drop
Feel guilty that others don't have this
Immediately think about what's still not right
Feel like a fraud who got lucky
'Happiness always costs you something'
'You don't deserve what you have'
'If it's not perfect, it's garbage'
'They'll figure out you're faking it'
I leave before I'm left
I make myself small so no one envies me
I overwork to compensate for feeling not enough
I stay quiet so no one tests what I know

q: The belief you need to unlearn:
options: 'Good things don't happen to people like me'
'I have to earn love'
'Anything less than perfect is failure'
'I only got here because no one looked closely'
